revisionHistory.description: Initial Release based on ERTMS/ETCS FRS v 5.00 dated 21 June 2007
revisionHistory.author: SRA System

introduction.purpose: The primary purpose of the ertms Functional Requirements Specification is to define the operational requirements for the EUROPEAN RAIL TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT SYSTEM / EUROPEAN TRAIN CONTROL SYSTEM (ERTMS/ETCS). This document outlines the fundamental functionalities necessary for safe and efficient train operations across European railway networks. It serves as a foundational reference for the development and implementation of the ertms system, ensuring consistency and adherence to specified operational parameters.
introduction.documentConventions: This document adheres to the guidelines set forth by IEEE 830-1998, Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications. Functional requirements are clearly identified, and a standardized approach to defining operational capabilities is maintained. Key terms and acronyms are defined in the Glossary to ensure clarity and common understanding among all stakeholders involved in the ertms project. Specific requirements are marked as (M) for Mandatory or (O) for Optional, indicating their criticality and implementation flexibility.
introduction.intendedAudience: The intended audience for this ertms Functional Requirements Specification includes system architects, software developers, quality assurance engineers, project managers, and railway operational staff. Additionally, regulatory bodies and certification authorities will find this document essential for evaluating compliance and ensuring the safety and interoperability of the ertms system. Stakeholders involved in future enhancements or maintenance of the system will also utilize this specification as a definitive reference.
introduction.productScope: The scope of the ertms system encompasses the full spectrum of train control and supervision functionalities, ranging from basic train movement information provision to advanced speed and distance supervision. It supports various application levels (Level 0, Level 1, Level 2, Level 3, Level STM) and manages complex operational states such as Full Supervision, Partial Supervision, and Shunting. The system interacts with trackside infrastructure and onboard equipment, processes national values, and ensures compatibility with existing national train control systems. It explicitly excludes detailed technical design specifications, focusing solely on the functional and operational requirements.
references: IEEE 830-1998 IEEE Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering.

overallDescription.productPerspective: The **ertms** system functions as a critical component within the broader European railway infrastructure, designed to enhance safety, interoperability, and operational efficiency. It serves as a replacement or upgrade for disparate national train control systems, aiming to standardize train control across borders. As a self-contained product, **ertms** integrates with existing railway signaling infrastructure while providing advanced train supervision capabilities. It interacts with both trackside equipment and onboard train systems, forming a cohesive unit for comprehensive traffic management.
productFunctions: Provision of driver information for safe train operation.
Supervision of train and shunting movements.
Management of multiple ETCS application levels (Level 0, 1, 2, 3, STM).
Handling of level transitions, including driver acknowledgement and automatic brake application.
Compatibility with existing national train control systems.
Supervision of various operational states: Full Supervision, Partial Supervision, Shunting, Unfitted Line, National operation.
Reception and application of national and default values from trackside.
Onboard equipment self-test and fault indication.
Management of train and driver data entry, storage, and consultation.
Dynamic and static train speed profile calculation and supervision.
Release speed calculation based on safety distances and odometry.
Accurate determination and transmission of train location.
Display of critical operational information on the Driver-Machine Interface (DMI).

userClassesAndCharacteristics.characteristics: Train Drivers are the primary operators of the **ertms** system, interacting directly via the Driver-Machine Interface (DMI). They possess varying levels of technical expertise but require clear, concise, and timely information to make critical operational decisions. Drivers are responsible for acknowledging level transitions, entering train and driver data, and responding to system warnings and fault indications. Their safety and operational efficiency are paramount to the system's design.

This class includes personnel responsible for managing train movements, scheduling, and overall railway network operations. They interact indirectly with the **ertms** system through its operational data and reports, ensuring adherence to timetables and responding to incidents. They require a high-level overview of system status and specific train locations, but typically do not operate the onboard equipment directly.

Infrastructure Managers are responsible for maintaining the trackside equipment and the Radio Block Centre (**RBC**). They configure and manage trackside data, transmit movement authorities, and monitor train movements within their jurisdiction. These users possess advanced technical knowledge of railway signaling and communications systems, focusing on system configuration, diagnostics, and intervention in case of anomalies.

overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: Software components operate within real-time constraints, performing complex calculations for speed profiles and braking curves. The system coexists with existing national train control systems, requiring seamless integration and non-interference. It is assumed that the underlying operating systems for both onboard and trackside components are real-time capable and highly robust, designed to withstand environmental factors typical of railway operations, including vibrations, temperature extremes, and electromagnetic interference.
designAndImplementationConstraints: The system shall adhere strictly to European railway standards and regulatory policies, including the CCS TSI, to ensure interoperability and safety across different national networks.
Hardware limitations, such as memory constraints and processing power on trainborne equipment, must be considered in the design of algorithms and data structures.
The **ertms** system must integrate with various existing national train control systems via Specific Transmission Modules (**STM**), ensuring compatibility without interference.

Specific technologies and communication protocols, such as radio for continuous transmission (Level 2/3) and balises/loops for intermittent transmission (Level 1), must be utilized as defined by ETCS specifications.
Rigorous security considerations must be applied, especially for key management and data integrity, to prevent unauthorized access or manipulation of critical operational data.
The system design must support parallel operations, allowing multiple trains to operate concurrently within supervised sections while maintaining safety separation and movement authority.
The Driver-Machine Interface (**DMI**) design shall conform to specified ergonomic and safety standards, ensuring clarity and minimizing cognitive load for drivers under operational stress.
userDocumentation: Comprehensive User Manuals for Train Drivers, detailing operation of the Driver-Machine Interface (DMI), interpretation of indications, and procedures for data entry and acknowledgements.
Maintenance Manuals for Onboard Equipment, providing instructions for self-test procedures, fault diagnosis, isolation of the ETCS trainborne equipment, and routine maintenance.

System Administrator Guides for Infrastructure Managers and Radio Block Centre (RBC) personnel, covering configuration of trackside data, management of national values, and monitoring of system status.
Troubleshooting Guides for common operational issues, including failures in transmission, onboard equipment faults, and recovery procedures from brake applications.
assumptionsAndDependencies: It is assumed that underlying trackside infrastructure (e.g., balises, loops, radio antennae) is correctly installed and maintained to transmit accurate and timely information to the **ertms** onboard equipment.

Dependencies exist on external railway management systems for automatic input of train data, although manual entry is also supported. The availability and accuracy of these external systems are critical.
The **ertms** system relies on the continued development and standardization of ETCS SRS and lower-level mandatory specifications, as changes in these documents may necessitate updates to the system.
It is assumed that trained personnel are available for the operation, maintenance, and administration of the **ertms** system, including drivers, maintenance technicians, and infrastructure managers.
The stability and reliability of communication networks (e.g., GSM-R) are critical dependencies for continuous transmission of information in Level 2 and Level 3 operations.

externalInterfaceRequirements.userInterfaces: The **ertms** system primarily interacts with users through the Driver-Machine Interface (**DMI**), which serves as the central control and display unit for train drivers. The DMI shall present critical operational information, including current speed, permitted speed, target distance, application level, and operational state, using clear, standardized graphical elements and indications. User input for train data entry, driver identification, and acknowledgement of level transitions shall be facilitated through a robust and intuitive interface, ensuring minimal distraction and maximum legibility under diverse lighting conditions. Error messages and fault indications shall be displayed prominently and unambiguously, guiding the driver through necessary actions. The DMI shall also be capable of displaying or being compatible with information originating from national train control systems, ensuring a consistent operational view.
externalInterfaceRequirements.hardwareInterfaces: The **ertms** system requires several critical hardware interfaces to perform its functions effectively. Onboard, it interfaces with the train's braking system, enabling automatic brake applications when supervision limits are exceeded or driver acknowledgements are missed. It also connects to odometry sensors to accurately determine train speed and location, which is crucial for calculating braking curves and managing movement authorities. Trackside, the system interfaces with balises and induction loops for intermittent data transmission and with radio communication modules for continuous data exchange in higher application levels. These interfaces must support high data integrity and reliability, operating robustly in harsh railway environments, including extreme temperatures, vibrations, and electromagnetic interference.
externalInterfaceRequirements.softwareInterfaces: The **ertms** system interfaces with several key software components and external systems. It communicates with the Radio Block Centre (**RBC**) software for continuous track-to-train and train-to-track information exchange, particularly in Level 2 and Level 3 operations. This includes receiving movement authorities and transmitting train location data. Integration with Specific Transmission Modules (**STM**) software is required to ensure compatibility with existing national train control systems, allowing the **ertms** onboard equipment to receive and interpret national system information. Furthermore, there may be interfaces with railway management systems for automatic input of train data, requiring well-defined **REST API**s or similar protocols for data exchange. All software interfaces must be robust, secure, and capable of handling real-time data flow without degradation of performance or safety.
externalInterfaceRequirements.communicationsInterfaces: The **ertms** system mandates robust communication interfaces for reliable data exchange. For intermittent transmission, it utilizes **balises** and **loops** embedded in the track, which communicate with antennas on the trainborne equipment to provide location updates and movement authority segments. For continuous communication, especially in Level 2 and 3, the system relies on **radio** communication, typically **GSM-R**, to exchange real-time data between the trainborne equipment and the Radio Block Centre (**RBC**). These communication links must ensure high availability, low latency, and secure data transfer. Pertinent message formatting shall adhere to ETCS communication protocols, ensuring standardized data packets and reliable interpretation. Specific requirements for communication security, data transfer rates, and synchronization mechanisms are critical to maintain the integrity and safety of train operations.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Operational Mode Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature provides the core functionality for the **ertms** system to manage and transition between various operational states and application levels, ensuring appropriate supervision and driver interaction based on trackside conditions and train configuration. It dynamically adapts the system's behavior to enhance safety and efficiency across different railway environments, from unfitted lines to full supervision areas. The system supports a range of levels, from Level 0 to Level 3, including integration with Specific Transmission Modules (**STM**) for national operations. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System powers on. Response: ETCS onboard equipment performs an automatic self-test and enters Stand By operation.
Stimulus: Driver attempts to select Shunting operation while train is moving. Response: System prevents selection and may initiate a warning.

Stimulus: Train enters a section of track requiring a higher ETCS application level. Response: The system automatically initiates a transition to the highest equipped level and indicates it on the DMI.
Stimulus: Trackside requests a level transition that increases driver responsibility. Response: The DMI displays a request for driver acknowledgement.
Stimulus: Driver fails to acknowledge a required level transition within the specified time. Response: The ETCS initiates a brake application.
Stimulus: Trainborne equipment is no longer operative. Response: The system exits the current supervision mode (Partial or Full) or Shunting.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-001: The system shall be capable of supervising the following operational states: Full Supervision, Partial Supervision, Staff Responsible, On Sight, Unfitted Line, Train Trip, Post Trip, National operation (STM), Tandem operation, Multiple operation, Shunting operation, Stand By operation, Reversing operation.

E-REQ-002: The system shall provide Level 0 functionality where ETCS is active for limited train control functions, and trackside is not fitted with any train control system or no STM is available onboard.

E-REQ-003: The system shall provide Level 1 functionality with basic track to train information via intermittent transmission media (e.g., balises), optionally supported by infill via balise, loop, or radio.
E-REQ-004: The system shall provide Level 2 functionality with basic track to train and train to track information via continuous radio transmission, with train detection provided by trackside.
E-REQ-005: The system shall provide Level 3 functionality identical to Level 2, but with train integrity provided by onboard equipment, making trackside train detection optional.

E-REQ-006: The system shall support Level STM (Specific Transmission Module) where track to train information is provided by a national system, and onboard functions are provided by the national STM in cooperation with onboard ETCS.
E-REQ-007: The system shall allow implementation of one or more ETCS application levels on a single line.
E-REQ-008: The system shall enable trains equipped for Level 3 to run on lines equipped with Level 3, 2, 1, and 0.
E-REQ-009: The system shall enable trains equipped for Level 2 to run on lines equipped with Level 2, 1, and 0.
E-REQ-010: The system shall enable trains equipped for Level 1 to run on lines equipped with Level 1 and 0.
E-REQ-011: The system shall indicate the current application level on the DMI.
E-REQ-012: The system shall request driver acknowledgement for level transitions if requested from trackside.
E-REQ-013: The system shall apply the brake if the driver does not acknowledge a requested level transition.
E-REQ-014: The system shall release the brake if the driver acknowledges a level transition after brake application.
E-REQ-015: The system shall, in principle, ensure automatic transitions between operational states while the train is moving.
E-REQ-016: The system shall initiate transitions between operational states automatically or manually as appropriate when the train is stationary.
E-REQ-017: The system shall seek driver acknowledgement if an automatic transition (except to/from National Operation STM) increases driver responsibility, regardless of train movement.
E-REQ-018: The system shall request driver acknowledgement for transitions to and from National Operation (STM).
E-REQ-019: The system shall initiate a brake application if driver acknowledgement is required for a transition and the driver fails to acknowledge.
E-REQ-020: The system shall ensure that during a transition period between two operational states, the supervision provides at least the same protection as the least restrictive state.
E-REQ-021: The system shall switch to the highest equipped ETCS level for which it is equipped, according to trackside priority, when passing a level transition to a line fitted with multiple levels.
E-REQ-022: The system shall initiate a brake application if the train passes a level transition to one or more levels for which it is not equipped.
E-REQ-023: The system shall indicate the current operational status to the driver on the DMI.
E-REQ-024: The system shall allow an ETCS equipped traction unit to be moved in Shunting without train data, track data, or movement authority.
E-REQ-025: The system shall only permit transfer to Shunting on driver's selection when the train is stationary.
E-REQ-026: The system shall obtain permission from the **RBC** for transfer to Shunting if the train is operating under RBC control to prevent unauthorized use.
E-REQ-027: The system shall indicate permission received for Shunting to the driver.
E-REQ-028: The system shall allow manual selection of Shunting from Stand By, Full Supervision, or Partial Supervision operation.

E-REQ-029: The system shall allow automatic transfer to Shunting from Full Supervision and Partial Supervision at any speed lower than or equal to the supervised shunting speed based on trackside information.
E-REQ-030: The system shall request confirmation from the driver before automatic transition to Shunting.
E-REQ-031: The system shall supervise Shunting operation to a permitted national speed value.
E-REQ-032: The system shall indicate the supervised Shunting speed to the driver on request.
E-REQ-033: The system shall apply the train trip function if a shunting movement passes a signal showing 'danger for shunting'.
E-REQ-034: The system shall only permit exit from Shunting when the train is stationary.
E-REQ-035: The system shall exit from Shunting when the driver selects exit from shunting.
E-REQ-036: The system shall allow Partial Supervision to be selected either by the driver or by information received from track-to-train transmission.
E-REQ-037: The system shall require the driver to acknowledge transfer from Full Supervision to Partial Supervision within 5 seconds if acknowledgement is specified.
E-REQ-038: The system shall indicate Partial Supervision on the DMI.
E-REQ-039: The system shall supervise the train in Partial Supervision according to available train speed and distance data.
E-REQ-040: The system shall have the capability to supervise the train to a ceiling speed in Partial Supervision.
E-REQ-041: The system shall not continually show the ceiling speed for Partial Supervision on the DMI, but may show it momentarily when selected by the driver.
E-REQ-042: The system shall exit Partial Supervision when the trainborne equipment is no longer operative, Shunting is selected, or Full Supervision is available.
E-REQ-043: The system shall allow a train trip to be ordered when passing a stop signal in Partial Supervision.
E-REQ-044: The system shall automatically transfer to Full Supervision when a movement authority and all other necessary information are received through track-to-train transmission.
E-REQ-045: The system shall enable the trackside to request driver confirmation regarding track occupancy ahead before sending a Full Supervision movement authority.
E-REQ-046: The system shall provide supervision of speed and distance in Full Supervision.
E-REQ-047: The system shall remain in Full Supervision until the trainborne equipment is no longer active, Shunting is selected, or Partial Supervision information is received.
E-REQ-048: The system shall allow Unfitted Line operation if ordered by trackside.
E-REQ-049: The system shall allow Unfitted Line operation if selected by the driver at start up.
E-REQ-050: The system shall supervise the train against a ceiling speed during Unfitted Line operation.
E-REQ-051: The system shall determine the ceiling speed for Unfitted Line operation by the lower value of maximum train speed and the National value for unfitted operation.
E-REQ-052: The system shall be capable of switching to another ETCS status when transmitted from trackside during Unfitted Line operation.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Train and Driver Data Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature manages all aspects of train and driver data, including entry, storage, and retrieval, which is essential for the accurate and safe operation of the **ertms** system. It ensures that crucial parameters like train identification, dimensions, braking characteristics, and driver credentials are correctly recorded and utilized. The system prioritizes safety by requiring data entry before movement and offers flexibility through both manual and automatic data provision. Data integrity is maintained by storing information until the traction unit is inoperative. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: System starts up. Response: The system requires train data entry before allowing train movement.
Stimulus: Driver selects 'Train Data Entry' on the DMI. Response: The DMI displays fields for entering or confirming train and driver data.
Stimulus: Driver enters new train data while the train is moving. Response: The system prevents manual entry or overwriting and displays a message requiring the train to be stationary.
Stimulus: Train data is automatically received from a railway management system. Response: The **ertms** onboard equipment processes and stores the received data.
Stimulus: Driver requests to consult train data. Response: The DMI displays current train data, irrespective of train movement status.
Stimulus: ETCS onboard fails to contact the **RBC** during awakening. Response: The DMI prompts the driver to enter RBC contact details.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-053: The system shall require train data to be entered before the onboard ETCS equipment allows train movement.
E-REQ-054: The driver shall be able to select Train Data Entry on the DMI.
E-REQ-055: The system shall allow entering or overwriting data manually by the driver only when stationary.
E-REQ-056: The system may allow train data to be entered automatically from a railway management system or from train memory.
E-REQ-057: The driver shall be able to consult train data when the train is stationary or moving.
E-REQ-058: The system shall store current train data (except at transition to shunting) in the ETCS equipment until the traction unit is not operative.
E-REQ-059: The system shall offer stored train data to the driver for confirmation when Data Entry starts.
E-REQ-060: The system for Train Data Entry shall provide for the input of other data required by STMs connected to ETCS, potentially including additional non-ETCS items.
E-REQ-061: The system shall allow the entry of driver identification and the selection of the language.
E-REQ-062: The system shall allow the change of driver identification or Train Running Number during a journey.
E-REQ-063: Following successful completion of Train Data Entry, the driver shall be able to perform shunting movements or train movements.

E-REQ-064: The system shall allow the following data to be entered manually by the driver or from train memory, or provided by external sources: Driver identification, Train identification (train number), STM ready for use, Data required for brake calculation, Maximum train speed, Train length, Status of air tight system, Type of electric power accepted, Data additional required for STM (if any), International train category, Train gauge, Maximum axle load of the train with a resolution of 0.5 t.
E-REQ-065: If the onboard fails to contact the **RBC** when awakening, the driver shall be asked to enter the **RBC** contact details.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Train Control and Supervision
systemFeatures.description: This feature encapsulates the core safety functionalities of the **ertms** system, including continuous supervision of train movements, calculation of safe speed profiles, determination of train location, and accurate display of speed information to the driver. It ensures that the train operates within authorized limits, automatically applying brakes if necessary to prevent unsafe conditions. The system performs complex real-time computations to maintain a dynamic and static speed profile, vital for preventing overspeeding and ensuring timely stops. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Train approaches a speed restriction or an end of movement authority. Response: ETCS calculates and enforces dynamic braking curves to ensure the train complies with the speed requirements.
Stimulus: Trainborne equipment detects a failure to apply the full service brake. Response: The emergency brake is applied to stop the train before the danger point.
Stimulus: Train travels over a balise or continuously transmits position to **RBC**. Response: The ETCS onboard equipment determines and updates the location of the entire train.
Stimulus: Driver observes speed on the DMI. Response: The DMI displays the actual speed, which is identical to the speed used for movement authority supervision.
Stimulus: Train reaches a location where a higher speed is permitted. Response: The rear end of the train respects the static train speed profile before accelerating.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-066: The system shall provide the driver with information to allow him to drive the train safely.
E-REQ-067: The system shall be able to supervise train and shunting movements.
E-REQ-068: The system shall, if supervision is performed by a **RBC**, be able to prevent movements of a traction unit in its area if not authorised by the **RBC**.
E-REQ-069: The system shall be functional to a maximum train speed of 500 km/h.
E-REQ-070: The system shall collect all relevant information concerning train and line speed.
E-REQ-071: The system shall calculate the permitted speed for the train for all locations of the authorised movement.
E-REQ-072: The system shall ensure that the static train speed profile also respects maximum line speed, track speed, and special speed levels for specific classes of trains.
E-REQ-073: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall calculate the static train speed profile on the basis of infrastructure data and train data.
E-REQ-074: Based on all relevant data, the ETCS shall calculate an emergency braking curve and a service braking curve.
E-REQ-075: The system shall allow the service brake intervention to be permitted or inhibited by trackside.
E-REQ-076: When changing to a lower speed level, the front end of the train shall respect the dynamic train speed profile.
E-REQ-077: When changing to a higher speed level, the rear end of the train shall respect the static train speed profile.
E-REQ-078: The system shall allow defining certain locations (e.g., tunnels) where speed increase is related to the front of the train.
E-REQ-079: The braking curves shall ensure that the train complies with its speed requirements.
E-REQ-080: Where failure to apply the full service brake is detected, the emergency brake shall stop the train in rear of the danger point.
E-REQ-081: The system shall calculate the release speed onboard, based on safety distance, overlap, accuracy of odometry, and deceleration performance of the train, or receive it from the trackside.
E-REQ-082: The release speed given from the trackside shall take priority over the release speed calculated onboard.
E-REQ-083: The system shall indicate the release speed on the DMI.
E-REQ-084: If the release speed is calculated onboard, it shall ensure that the train will stop before reaching the danger point.

E-REQ-085: When the train is stationary or after a certain time, the release speed calculation shall be based on the distance to the danger point (if calculated onboard); the condition for this change shall be defined as infrastructure data.
E-REQ-086: Each railway shall have the possibility of allowing a different release speed for every signal.
E-REQ-087: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be able to determine the location of the entire train.
E-REQ-088: On lines fitted with **RBC**, the ETCS trainborne equipment shall be able to transmit the location of the entire train to the **RBC**.
E-REQ-089: The train location calculation shall take into account error of odometry.
E-REQ-090: Actual speed shall be indicated on the DMI.
E-REQ-091: There shall be no discrepancy between the speed shown to the driver and the speed used for supervision of movement authorities and speed limits.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Infrastructure Data Processing
systemFeatures.description: This feature describes how the **ertms** system collects, processes, and utilizes data received from the trackside infrastructure to ensure safe and efficient train movement. It is responsible for interpreting track descriptions, adhesion conditions, and speed profile information, which are critical for the onboard equipment to calculate safe movement authorities and braking curves. This ensures that train operations are continuously adapted to the prevailing track conditions and operational directives provided by the railway infrastructure. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: Train passes a balise transmitting track description data. Response: The ETCS onboard equipment receives and processes the track description.
Stimulus: Trackside transmits updated adhesion conditions. Response: The ETCS onboard adjusts braking curve calculations based on the new adhesion data.
Stimulus: Onboard ETCS receives information for speed profile calculations. Response: The system calculates the permitted speed for the train across the authorized movement path.
Stimulus: Onboard ETCS attempts to calculate a movement authority without sufficient track data. Response: The system rejects the movement authority and prevents further movement.
Stimulus: Driver attempts to change adhesion conditions. Response: The system allows the change, but gives priority to information received from trackside.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-092: The ETCS onboard shall be capable of receiving track description from the trackside.
E-REQ-093: The system shall allow sending information on adhesion conditions from trackside.
E-REQ-094: The system shall allow the driver to change adhesion conditions, with trackside information having priority.
E-REQ-095: The trackside shall be able to send information for the calculation of speed profiles.
E-REQ-096: If track data at least to the location where the relevant movement authority ends are not available onboard, the movement authority shall be rejected.
E-REQ-097: Track to train transmission shall provide the capability to send different speed profiles for specific train categories.
E-REQ-098: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall supervise the end of movement authority if this information is available onboard.
E-REQ-099: The target distance to be displayed on the DMI shall be based on the most restrictive braking curve.

E-REQ-100: Together with the movement authority, the onboard shall be able to receive one or more time-out(s) for certain sections of the movement authority, and shorten the movement authority accordingly when a time out expires.

E-REQ-101: Using train data and infrastructure data, braking curves shall be calculated taking into account target information but not the location of vehicles occupying the track for On Sight operation.
E-REQ-102: The ceiling speed level for the movement authority in On Sight operation shall be defined as data National Value.
E-REQ-103: Before entering an occupied track, a driver acknowledgement shall be requested.
E-REQ-104: The system shall supervise the train in On Sight operation according to available train speed data.
E-REQ-105: The system shall, as a minimum, supervise the train to a ceiling speed in On Sight operation.
E-REQ-106: The supervised speed in On Sight operation shall not be shown on the DMI unless selected by the driver.
E-REQ-107: The target distance in On Sight operation shall not be shown on the DMI unless selected by the driver.
E-REQ-108: On request of the **RBC**, the driver shall have the possibility to confirm that the track ahead of him until the end of the on sight section is clear.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Interoperability and Compatibility
systemFeatures.description: This feature addresses the crucial requirements for the **ertms** system to operate seamlessly across different national railway networks and to coexist with existing train control systems. It ensures that the system can adapt to specific national requirements through the reception and application of national values, while maintaining a fallback mechanism with default values. This functionality is vital for achieving the goal of a harmonized European rail traffic management system. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **ertms** onboard equipment receives national values from trackside. Response: The system stores and applies the national values to adapt to national requirements.
Stimulus: **ertms** onboard equipment operates in an area without valid national values. Response: The system automatically reverts to using harmonized default values stored onboard.

Stimulus: Train equipped with **ertms** encounters an existing national train control system. Response: The **ertms** system operates without interfering with or being interfered by the national system.
Stimulus: **ertms** onboard receives information from a national train control system via an **STM**. Response: The DMI displays or is compatible with the information from the national system.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-109: The ETCS onboard shall be capable of receiving National values from the trackside to adapt to National requirements.
E-REQ-110: National values shall be applicable to a defined area.
E-REQ-111: Once received onboard, the national values shall remain valid even if the onboard equipment is switched off.
E-REQ-112: If the onboard has no valid national values for the current location, default values shall be used by the onboard equipment.
E-REQ-113: Default values shall be harmonised values, permanently stored in all **ertms** onboard equipment.

E-REQ-114: The ETCS shall be compatible with existing national systems listed in the CCS TSI such that it does not interfere with the national systems and is not interfered with by the national systems.
E-REQ-115: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of receiving information from the national train control systems by means of the **STM**.
E-REQ-116: The DMI shall display or be compatible with information from national train control systems, potentially by displaying the information shown by the national system.

systemFeatures.name: ertms Onboard Equipment Management
systemFeatures.description: This feature covers the fundamental management of the **ertms** onboard equipment, including its initial self-test upon startup and the ability to isolate the system when necessary. The self-test ensures that the equipment is operational and safe before train movement, providing critical feedback to the driver. Isolation functionality allows for safe disengagement of the **ertms** system, for example, during maintenance or in specific operational scenarios, while clearly indicating its isolated status. Priority: High.
stimulusResponseSequences: Stimulus: **ertms** onboard equipment powers on. Response: The equipment automatically performs a self-test without requiring driver action.
Stimulus: Self-test completes. Response: The DMI indicates the result of the self-test (e.g., pass/fail).
Stimulus: Driver initiates isolation of the **ertms** trainborne equipment. Response: The system disconnects from the vehicle braking system and the DMI shows only that the system is isolated.
Stimulus: **ertms** onboard equipment is in an isolated state. Response: No **ertms** operational information is displayed, only the 'isolated' status.
functionalRequirements: E-REQ-117: At Start Up, the onboard equipment shall perform an automatic self-test.
E-REQ-118: The self-test shall require no action on the part of the driver.
E-REQ-119: The DMI shall indicate the result of the self-test.
E-REQ-120: The ETCS trainborne equipment shall be capable of being isolated.
E-REQ-121: When the ETCS trainborne equipment is isolated, the system shall not show any ETCS information other than the fact that the system is isolated.
E-REQ-122: Isolation of the ETCS trainborne equipment shall disconnect the ETCS trainborne equipment from the vehicle braking system.

performanceRequirements: E-NFR-001: The **ertms** system shall maintain continuous supervision and perform all required calculations (speed profiles, braking curves, train location) in real-time to support train speeds up to 500 km/h with an update frequency sufficient to ensure safety. Rationale: High-speed operation necessitates extremely rapid response times for control and safety functions to prevent accidents and ensure operational fluidity.

E-NFR-002: The system shall execute brake applications within 0.5 seconds of a critical safety violation being detected, such as overspeed or missed driver acknowledgement. Rationale: Rapid intervention is essential to prevent trains from exceeding safe limits or entering unauthorized sections, directly impacting overall system safety.

E-NFR-003: Communication latency for critical track-to-train and train-to-track messages (e.g., movement authorities, train location updates) shall not exceed 300 milliseconds end-to-end. Rationale: Low latency is crucial for accurate and timely information exchange, enabling dynamic route management and real-time supervision, especially in high-density traffic areas.
safetyRequirements: E-NFR-004: The **ertms** system shall be designed and implemented to meet Safety Integrity Level 4 (SIL4) according to EN 50126, EN 50128, and EN 50129 standards. Rationale: SIL4 is the highest safety integrity level for railway applications, mandatory for systems whose failure could lead to catastrophic consequences, such as collisions or derailments.

E-NFR-005: The system shall employ fail-safe mechanisms to ensure that in the event of any detected internal failure or external communication loss, the system defaults to a safe state, typically initiating an emergency brake application. Rationale: This prevents uncontrolled train movements and ensures that system malfunctions do not compromise safety.

E-NFR-006: All critical safety messages exchanged between onboard and trackside components shall incorporate robust error detection and correction mechanisms (e.g., CRC, sequence numbers) to prevent data corruption or spoofing. Rationale: Data integrity is paramount for preventing erroneous movement authorities or supervision decisions that could lead to dangerous situations.
securityRequirements: E-NFR-007: The **ertms** system shall implement strong authentication mechanisms for driver identification and authorization to prevent unauthorized operation of trains. Rationale: Unauthorized access could lead to misuse, operational errors, or intentional malicious acts, compromising safety and system integrity.

E-NFR-008: All critical communication links, particularly radio-based (GSM-R) for Level 2 and Level 3, shall employ cryptographic protocols to ensure confidentiality and integrity of exchanged data. Rationale: Protection against eavesdropping, tampering, and replay attacks is essential to prevent external interference with train control commands and operational data.

E-NFR-009: Key management procedures for cryptographic keys used in secure communications shall adhere to industry best practices, including secure generation, distribution, storage, and revocation. Rationale: Effective key management is fundamental to maintaining the overall security posture of the encrypted communication channels, protecting against compromise.
softwareQualityAttributes: E-NFR-010: Reliability: The **ertms** system shall demonstrate an availability of 99.999% (five nines) for its core safety-critical functions, with a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of at least 100,000 hours for onboard equipment. Rationale: High reliability is essential for continuous safe operation, minimizing service disruptions and maintaining public trust in the railway system.

E-NFR-011: Maintainability: The system architecture shall be modular and well-documented, allowing for efficient diagnosis, repair, and updates of software components with minimal downtime. Rationale: Ease of maintenance reduces operational costs, facilitates bug fixes, and supports future enhancements, extending the system's lifespan.

E-NFR-012: Testability: All functional and non-functional requirements shall be verifiable through automated and manual testing procedures, with comprehensive test coverage for all operational modes and interfaces. Rationale: High testability ensures that the system can be thoroughly validated against its specifications, increasing confidence in its safety and performance.

E-NFR-013: Usability: The Driver-Machine Interface (**DMI**) shall be designed for optimal human factors, ensuring intuitive operation, clear information presentation, and minimal cognitive load for drivers under high-stress conditions. Rationale: A highly usable DMI reduces the likelihood of driver error, contributing directly to operational safety and efficiency.
businessRules: E-BR-001: All requirements marked as (M) (Mandatory) in the specifications shall be fully implemented in every **ertms** application. Rationale: These requirements are fundamental to the core functionality, safety, and interoperability of the system as defined by European railway standards.

E-BR-002: Requirements marked as (O) (Optional) may be implemented based on specific operational needs or national railway policies, provided that if implemented, they adhere to applicable **ETCS SRS** and lower-level mandatory specifications. Rationale: This allows flexibility for national adaptations while ensuring compliance with overarching safety and functional standards.

E-BR-003: In scenarios where adhesion conditions are provided by both trackside and the driver, the trackside information shall always take precedence. Rationale: Trackside information is considered authoritative for environmental conditions, ensuring consistent and safer braking calculations.

E-BR-004: All driver actions that increase operational responsibility (e.g., specific level transitions, entering occupied tracks) must be explicitly acknowledged by the driver via the DMI. Rationale: This ensures driver awareness and accountability for critical operational decisions, serving as a vital safety check.

otherRequirements: E-OTH-001: The **ertms** system shall provide functionalities to support driver training scenarios, allowing for simulation of various operational states, level transitions, and incident responses without impacting live operations. Rationale: Effective training is crucial for driver proficiency and safety, and a dedicated training mode reduces the risks associated with learning on live systems.

E-OTH-002: The system shall incorporate mechanisms for logging all critical operational events, including driver actions, system warnings, fault indications, and communication messages, for post-incident analysis and system diagnostics. Rationale: Comprehensive logging is essential for forensic analysis, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement of system performance and safety.

E-OTH-003: The **ertms** system shall support remote diagnostics capabilities for onboard equipment, allowing maintenance personnel to assess system health and retrieve diagnostic data without direct physical access to the train. Rationale: Remote diagnostics streamline maintenance processes, reduce train downtime, and improve overall system availability.

glossary.definition: Control Command and Signalling Technical Specification for Interoperability. A European standard defining technical specifications for railway control, command, and signalling systems.
Driver-Machine Interface. The display and control unit through which the train driver interacts with the ETCS onboard equipment.
European Railway Agency. Now known as the European Union Agency for Railways (ERA), responsible for railway safety and interoperability in Europe.
European Rail Traffic Management System. An initiative to create a single European standard for railway signaling, comprising ETCS and GSM-R.
European Train Control System. The signaling and train protection component of ERTMS, providing train drivers with movement authority and supervising train speed.
Functional Requirements Specification. A document that describes the functions that a system or component must be able to perform.
Global System for Mobile Communications – Railway. An international wireless communications standard for railway communication and applications.
A requirement that must be respected and implemented in every ETCS application.
A function that is not mandatory to implement in every ETCS application, but if implemented, must respect applicable ETCS SRS and lower-level mandatory specifications.
Radio Block Centre. The trackside entity in ETCS Level 2 and Level 3 that calculates and transmits movement authorities to trains via radio.
System Requirements Specification. A detailed document outlining the requirements for a software system.
Specific Transmission Module. An onboard component that allows ETCS-equipped trains to operate on lines fitted with existing national train control systems.
An automatic brake application initiated by the ETCS system due to a safety violation or operational constraint.

flowchartDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This flowchart illustrates the high-level process for ertms Operational State Transitions. Nodes represent states or processes, and diamond shapes represent decisions. Arrows indicate transitions between states. The direction of the flow is from top to bottom (TD). Node IDs are alphanumeric and unique. Labels with spaces are enclosed in double quotes.
flowchartDiagram.code:
flowchart TD
  StartProcess["ertms System Start Up"] --> SelfTest["Onboard Self-Test"]
  SelfTest --> SelfTestResult{"Self-Test Successful?"}
  SelfTestResult -- No --> FaultIndication["Indicate Fault on DMI"]
  FaultIndication --> EndProcess["System Halted / Repair Required"]
  SelfTestResult -- Yes --> StandBy["Stand By Operation"]
  StandBy --> DriverDataEntry["Driver Data Entry Required?"]
  DriverDataEntry -- Yes --> TrainDataInput["Train Data Input (Manual / Auto)"]
  TrainDataInput --> DataInputConfirm{"Data Validated?"}
  DataInputConfirm -- No --> TrainDataInput
  DataInputConfirm -- Yes --> InitialModeSelect["Initial Mode Selection"]
  InitialModeSelect --> ShuntingMode{"Shunting Selected?"}
  ShuntingMode -- Yes --> RequestRBC["Request RBC Permission (if under RBC control)"]
  RequestRBC --> ShuntPermit{"Permission Granted?"}
  ShuntPermit -- No --> InitialModeSelect
  ShuntPermit -- Yes --> ShuntingOperation["Shunting Operation"]
  ShuntingOperation --> ShuntExit["Exit Shunting (Stationary)"]
  InitialModeSelect -- No --> LevelTransitionCheck{"Trackside Level Transition?"}
  LevelTransitionCheck -- Yes --> AckRequired{"Ack. Required?"}
  AckRequired -- Yes --> DriverAcknowledge["Driver Acknowledge on DMI"]
  DriverAcknowledge --> AckOutcome{"Acknowledged?"}
  AckOutcome -- No --> BrakeApply["Brake Application"]
  AckOutcome -- Yes --> ModeSupervision["Full / Partial Supervision"]
  LevelTransitionCheck -- No --> ModeSupervision
  ShuntExit --> StandBy
  ModeSupervision --> EventLoop["Operational Event Loop"]
  EventLoop -- "Level Change" --> LevelTransitionCheck
  EventLoop -- "Driver Request" --> InitialModeSelect
  ModeSupervision --> IsolationReq{"Isolation Requested?"}
  IsolationReq -- Yes --> IsolateSystem["Isolate ETCS Onboard"]
  IsolateSystem --> IsolatedStatus["Indicate Isolated on DMI"]
  IsolatedStatus --> EndProcess
flowchartDiagram.caption: ertms Operational State Transition Flow

sequenceDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This sequence diagram illustrates the interaction flow for a Driver Initiating Train Data Entry and subsequent Movement Authorization in the ertms system. Participants include the Driver, the Driver-Machine Interface (DMI), the ETCS Onboard Unit, and the Radio Block Centre (RBC). Standard synchronous and asynchronous messages are used to show the flow of information and control. Blocks like 'alt' (alternative) are used for conditional paths. Participant aliases are not quoted.
sequenceDiagram.code:
sequenceDiagram
  participant D as Driver
  participant M as DMI
  participant E as "ETCS Onboard"
  participant R as RBC

  D->>M: "Select 'Train Data Entry'"
  M->>E: "Request Train Data Form"
  E->>M: "Display Train Data Form"

  D->>M: "Enter Train & Driver Data (e.g., ID, Length)"
  M->>E: "Submit Train & Driver Data"

  E->>E: "Validate Data (Stationary Check, RBC Contact)"
  alt Data Valid & RBC Contact OK
    E->>R: "Transmit Driver & Train ID"
    R-->>E: "RBC Confirmation / Authorisation"
    E->>E: "Store Validated Data"
    E->>M: "Display 'Data Entry Successful'"
    D->>M: "Acknowledge Data Entry"
    M->>E: "Driver Acknowledged"
    E->>E: "Enable Train Movement"
    E->>M: "Display 'Ready for Movement'"
    D->>E: "Initiate Movement"
  else Data Invalid or RBC Contact Failed
    E->>M: "Display 'Error: Invalid Data / RBC Contact Failed'"
    D->>M: "Re-enter Data or Enter RBC Details"
  end
sequenceDiagram.caption: Driver Data Entry and Movement Sequence

entityRelationshipDiagram.syntaxExplanation: This ER Diagram models key entities within the ertms system, including Train, Driver, ETCS Onboard, Operational State, National Value, Trackside Information, and Radio Block Centre (RBC). It defines relationships and cardinalities between entities, along with primary keys (PK) and foreign keys (FK) to represent database schema design. Attribute names are alphanumeric and not quoted. Entity names with spaces are quoted. Only PK, FK, UK keys are used.
entityRelationshipDiagram.code:
erDiagram
    DRIVER ||--o{ TRAIN : "operates"
    TRAIN ||--|{ ETCS_ONBOARD : "equipped_with"
    ETCS_ONBOARD ||--|{ OPERATIONAL_STATE : "manages"
    ETCS_ONBOARD ||--o{ NATIONAL_VALUE : "receives_from_trackside"
    "TRACKSIDE_INFO" ||--|{ NATIONAL_VALUE : "defines"
    "TRACKSIDE_INFO" ||--o{ TRAIN : "provides_data_to"
    ETCS_ONBOARD ||--o{ RBC : "communicates_with"

    DRIVER {
        int driver_id PK
        string name
        string identification_number UK
        string current_language
    }

    TRAIN {
        int train_id PK
        string train_number UK
        decimal max_speed
        decimal length
        decimal max_axle_load
        string gauge
        int etcs_onboard_id FK
    }

    ETCS_ONBOARD {
        int etcs_onboard_id PK
        string firmware_version
        string operational_status
        datetime last_self_test
        int current_operational_state_id FK
    }

    OPERATIONAL_STATE {
        int state_id PK
        string state_name UK
        string description
    }

    NATIONAL_VALUE {
        int value_id PK
        string value_name UK
        string value_data
        string applicable_area
        int trackside_info_id FK
    }

    "TRACKSIDE_INFO" {
        int trackside_info_id PK
        string location_identifier UK
        string track_description
        string adhesion_conditions
    }

    RBC {
        int rbc_id PK
        string rbc_name UK
        string contact_details
    }
